2fa.description: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app.

reconfigure_2fa.title: Two-Factor Authentication
reconfigure_2fa.description: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app.

reauth_confirm.title: Re-authenticate Aegis for Ajax
reauth_confirm.description: Your Ajax session for {email} is no longer valid. Enter the current account password to restore access.

reauth_2fa.title: Two-Factor Authentication
reauth_2fa.description: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app.

error.invalid_totp: Invalid TOTP code. Please try again.

abort.already_in_progress: Configuration is already in progress. Please complete or cancel the existing flow.
abort.account_locked: Account is locked. Please try again later.

data.force_arm: Force arm (ignore open sensors and malfunctions)
data.auto_create_labels: Auto-create and assign Aegis labels to entities

force_arm.name: Force arm
force_arm.description: Arm the system ignoring open sensors and active alarms.

entity_id.name: Entity
entity_id.description: Alarm panel entity to force arm. If omitted, arms all panels.

force_arm_night.name: Force arm night mode
force_arm_night.description: Arm night mode ignoring open sensors and active alarms.

entity_id.name: Entity
entity_id.description: Alarm panel entity to force arm night. If omitted, arms all panels.

press_panic_button.name: Press panic button (SOS)
press_panic_button.description: Trigger the Ajax SOS / panic button on a space. Calls the same endpoint as the red SOS button in the official mobile app and forwards a Panic alarm to the monitoring station (CRA), which on most contracts triggers immediate police dispatch and bypasses verification windows. Use only for genuine emergencies. The confirm field must be set to true or the call is rejected.

confirm.name: Confirm panic
confirm.description: Must be set to true. Acts as a safety lock to prevent automations from triggering this service accidentally.

entity_id.name: Entity
entity_id.description: Alarm panel entity for the space whose panic button to press. If omitted, the panic is sent to every configured space.

latitude.name: Latitude
latitude.description: Optional caller latitude forwarded to Ajax. Use with longitude.

longitude.name: Longitude
longitude.description: Optional caller longitude forwarded to Ajax. Use with latitude.

hub_offline_24h.title: Hub {hub_name} offline for over {hours_offline}h
hub_offline_24h.description: Aegis has been receiving an OFFLINE connection status for hub {hub_name} for {hours_offline} hours straight. Entities are stale until the hub reconnects. Common causes: hub unplugged, network outage, or the hub's outbound connection to the Ajax cloud is blocked by a firewall. The Repair clears automatically as soon as Aegis sees the hub back online.

hts_chronic_failure.title: Hub network sensors degraded
hts_chronic_failure.description: The HTS connection (which feeds the Ethernet / Wi-Fi / GSM / mains-power sensors) for space {space_id} has failed to stay up for {minutes_failing} minutes. Hub network sensors will be unavailable until it recovers. Most often caused by a firewall blocking outbound traffic to Ajax HTS endpoints, or by sustained network instability between Home Assistant and the Ajax cloud. The Repair clears automatically when HTS reconnects.

fcm_credentials_invalid.title: Push notifications disabled — FCM credentials rejected

init.title: Fix Aegis FCM credentials
init.description: Firebase Cloud Messaging registration failed for this account. The configured FCM Project ID / App ID / API Key / Sender ID are most likely wrong for your co-branded Ajax app, or the API key has been revoked. Re-enter the four values below and the integration will reload — if the new credentials work the Repair clears automatically; if not, the form re-appears. See the README's 'How to obtain FCM credentials' section.

abort.entry_missing: The Aegis account this Repair was raised for is no longer configured. Nothing to fix.

fcm_not_configured.title: Push notifications not configured — real-time events disabled

init.title: Configure Aegis FCM credentials
init.description: FCM credentials have never been set for this account, so real-time events from Ajax (doorbell ring, arm/disarm pushes, alarm) cannot reach Home Assistant — the integration falls back to polling every 5 minutes. Enter the four FCM values for your co-branded Ajax app below and the integration will reload. See the README's 'How to obtain FCM credentials' section for how to extract them from your Ajax app.

abort.entry_missing: The Aegis account this Repair was raised for is no longer configured. Nothing to fix.

grpcio_version_mismatch.title: Aegis needs grpcio {required} or newer (found {current})
grpcio_version_mismatch.description: Aegis depends on `grpcio` for the gRPC channel that talks to the Ajax cloud, and your Home Assistant install ships `grpcio` {current}. Login can fail with cryptic HTTP/2 errors below {required}. On Home Assistant Core or Container the integration manifest pins the version on install, but on Home Assistant OS the runtime ships system-wide grpcio that this manifest cannot influence — you'll typically need to upgrade Home Assistant OS to a build that includes a newer grpcio. The Repair clears automatically on the next reload once the version crosses {required}.
